cards.subject: torts
cards.statement: To recover for negligence, a plaintiff must prove duty, breach of that duty, actual causation, proximate causation, and damages.
distractors: causes a harmful or offensive contact with the plaintiff's person

cards.subject: torts
cards.statement: Battery requires an intentional act by the defendant that causes a harmful or offensive contact with the plaintiff's person.
cards.answer: causes a harmful or offensive contact with the plaintiff's person
distractors: out-of-court statement offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted

cards.subject: contracts
cards.statement: Consideration is defined as a bargained-for exchange in which each party incurs a legal detriment or confers a legal benefit.
cards.answer: legal detriment or confers a legal benefit
distractors: another human being committed with malice aforethought

cards.subject: evidence
cards.statement: Hearsay is defined as an out-of-court statement offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted, and it is inadmissible unless an exception applies.
cards.answer: out-of-court statement offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted

another human being committed with malice aforethought
legal detriment or confers a legal benefit

cards.subject: crim
cards.statement: Before a custodial interrogation, officers must warn the suspect of the right to remain silent, that any statement may be used against him, the right to counsel, and the right to appointed counsel if he cannot afford one.
distractors: another human being committed with malice aforethought

cards.subject: crim
cards.statement: Common law murder is defined as the unlawful killing of another human being committed with malice aforethought.
cards.answer: another human being committed with malice aforethought

cards.subject: realprop
cards.statement: Title by adverse possession requires possession that is actual, open and notorious, hostile, exclusive, and continuous throughout the statutory period.

cards.subject: conlaw
cards.statement: Under strict scrutiny, a law is upheld only if it is necessary to serve a compelling government interest, is narrowly tailored, and uses the least restrictive means available.
